title: Interpret Blue Status Indicators On The Container Selection Screen
summary: Use the Container Selection screen's blue visual indicators to determine whether a station or rack position has a tote present or is empty. The source states that dark blue indicates the presence of a tote and light blue represents an empty rack.
when_to_use: Use this reference when viewing the Container Selection screen and needing to interpret the meaning of the small blue indicator shown on a station or rack position.
not_for: Do not use this reference to infer any status meaning beyond the two documented values: tote present and empty rack.
Do not use this reference when the displayed indicator does not appear as dark blue or light blue as described in the source.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: View the Container Selection screen and interpret the blue indicator using the documented color meanings.

safety_notes: This source is a visual interpretation reference only and does not provide corrective actions.
Do not infer additional status meanings beyond tote present and empty rack because the source only documents those values.
access_or_tools_needed: Access to the Container Selection screen
Visual view of the blue status indicator on the display

access_required: Access to the Container Selection screen
system_preconditions: The Container Selection screen is open or visible.
The relevant station or rack position is visible on the display.

visual_references.description: Container Selection screen frame showing the blue indicator meaning for tote present versus empty rack.

steps.title: View the Container Selection screen
steps.purpose: Access the screen where the blue indicator is displayed.
steps.instruction: Open or view the Container Selection screen for totes or containers.
steps.expected_result: The Container Selection screen is visible.
steps.healthy_condition: The operator can see station or rack entries on the Container Selection display.
steps.failure_condition: The Container Selection screen is not available or the relevant entries are not visible.

actions.label: View Container Selection screen
actions.exact_action: Open or view the Container Selection screen for totes or containers.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Container Selection screen showing station or rack entries with blue indicators.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the Container Selection screen cannot be viewed.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: OCR shows 'Container Selection' and describes selecting a location to show a box icon and tote number, with rack or AGV location information.
Training slide frame showing the OptiSweep container selection view and how tote presence and location are indicated.

steps.title: Locate the blue indicator
steps.purpose: Identify the visual status marker associated with the relevant station or rack position.
steps.instruction: Locate the small blue indicator shown on top of the relevant station or position as described in the source.
steps.expected_result: The relevant blue indicator is identified on the screen.
steps.healthy_condition: A small blue indicator is visible for the station or rack position being checked.
steps.failure_condition: The indicator cannot be located or is not clearly visible.

actions.label: Find blue indicator
actions.exact_action: Locate the small blue indicator shown on top of the relevant station or position as described in the source.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The small blue indicator on top of some stations or positions.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the relevant indicator cannot be identified on the display.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Transcript mentions a small blue thing on top of some stations.

steps.title: Observe the indicator color
steps.purpose: Determine which documented color state is being displayed.
steps.instruction: Observe whether the indicator is dark blue or light blue.
steps.expected_result: The operator identifies the indicator as dark blue or light blue.
steps.healthy_condition: The indicator color can be clearly distinguished as one of the two documented blue states.
steps.failure_condition: The indicator color is unclear or does not match dark blue or light blue.

actions.label: Check indicator color
actions.exact_action: Observe whether the indicator is dark blue or light blue.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Examples of dark blue versus light blue indicator states on the Container Selection screen.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the displayed indicator does not match the documented dark blue or light blue meanings.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: OCR states dark blue indicates the presence of a tote and light blue represents an empty rack.

steps.title: Interpret the documented meaning
steps.purpose: Translate the observed color into the documented rack or tote status.
steps.instruction: Compare the observed color to the documented meaning: dark blue indicates the presence of a tote, and light blue represents an empty rack.
steps.expected_result: The operator determines whether a tote is present or the rack is empty.
steps.healthy_condition: Dark blue is interpreted as tote present, or light blue is interpreted as empty rack.
steps.failure_condition: The operator cannot map the observed color to one of the documented meanings.

actions.label: Interpret color meaning
actions.exact_action: Compare the observed color to the documented meaning: dark blue indicates the presence of a tote, and light blue represents an empty rack.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The documented legend or visual example indicating dark blue means tote present and light blue means empty rack.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the displayed indicator does not match the documented dark blue or light blue meanings.
Stop interpretation if additional status meanings would need to be inferred beyond tote present and empty rack.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Dark blue indicates the presence of a tote and light blue represents an empty rack.

steps.title: Communicate the interpreted status
steps.purpose: Share or retain the interpreted result for the selected station or rack position.
steps.instruction: Record or communicate the interpreted status for the selected station or rack position.
steps.expected_result: The interpreted status is available for operational use.
steps.healthy_condition: The station or rack position is identified as either tote present or empty rack based on the documented indicator meaning.
steps.failure_condition: The status cannot be confidently communicated because the indicator meaning is unclear or unsupported.

actions.label: Record interpreted status
actions.exact_action: Record or communicate the interpreted status for the selected station or rack position.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the interpreted status cannot be confidently determined from the documented dark blue and light blue meanings.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The source explains the indicator meanings needed to determine whether a tote is present or the rack is empty.

success_criteria: The user can determine from the Container Selection display whether a tote is present or whether the rack position is empty.
The observed indicator is interpreted using only the documented meanings: dark blue for tote present and light blue for empty rack.
healthy_conditions: The Container Selection screen is visible.
The small blue indicator can be located for the relevant station or rack position.
The indicator appears as dark blue or light blue.
Dark blue is interpreted as tote present.
Light blue is interpreted as empty rack.
failure_conditions: The Container Selection screen cannot be viewed.
The relevant blue indicator cannot be located.
The indicator color is unclear or does not match dark blue or light blue.
Additional status meaning would need to be inferred beyond what the source documents.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if the displayed indicator does not match the documented dark blue or light blue meanings.
Escalate if the indicator cannot be clearly seen or interpreted on the Container Selection screen.
Do not infer additional status meanings beyond tote present and empty rack because the source only documents those values.
